parameters.query:
WITH stale_jobs AS (
  UPDATE inbox.processing_jobs
  SET status = 'failed',
      run_after = now(),
      locked_at = NULL,
      locked_by = NULL,
      last_error = COALESCE(last_error, 'Recovered stale running job')
  WHERE status = 'running'
    AND locked_at < now() - interval '15 minutes'
  RETURNING id
),
exhausted_jobs AS (
  UPDATE inbox.processing_jobs
  SET status = 'cancelled',
      locked_at = NULL,
      locked_by = NULL,
      last_error = COALESCE(last_error, 'Maximum retry attempts reached')
  WHERE status IN ('pending', 'failed')
    AND attempts >= max_attempts
  RETURNING id
),
stale_items AS (
  UPDATE inbox.inbox_items
  SET status = 'received',
      retry_count = retry_count + 1,
      error_code = 'STALE_ANALYSIS_RECOVERED',
      error_message = 'Recovered by maintenance workflow'
  WHERE status = 'analyzing'
    AND updated_at < now() - interval '15 minutes'
    AND retry_count < 4
  RETURNING id
),
analysis_candidate AS (
  SELECT items.id
  FROM inbox.inbox_items AS items
  WHERE items.status IN ('received', 'failed')
    AND items.normalized_text IS NOT NULL
    AND items.retry_count < 4
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM inbox.knowledge_items knowledge WHERE knowledge.inbox_item_id = items.id)
  ORDER BY items.received_at
  LIMIT 1
),
index_candidate AS (
  SELECT jobs.inbox_item_id
  FROM inbox.processing_jobs AS jobs
  WHERE jobs.job_type = 'index'
    AND jobs.status IN ('pending', 'failed')
    AND jobs.attempts < jobs.max_attempts
    AND jobs.run_after <= now()
  ORDER BY jobs.created_at
  LIMIT 1
),
owner AS (
  SELECT telegram_user_id::text AS chat_id
  FROM inbox.users
  WHERE status = 'active' AND telegram_user_id IS NOT NULL
  ORDER BY created_at
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  COALESCE((SELECT id::text FROM analysis_candidate), '') AS analysis_item_id,
  COALESCE((SELECT inbox_item_id::text FROM index_candidate), '') AS index_inbox_item_id,
  COALESCE((SELECT chat_id FROM owner), '') AS alert_chat_id,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM stale_jobs) AS stale_jobs_recovered,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM stale_items) AS stale_items_recovered,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM exhausted_jobs) AS jobs_cancelled;
